text:
There once was a boy named Max. Max lived in the heart of the city but loved wandering the countryside, finding adventure among haystacks and alongside quiet rivers. One rainy autumn, he decided to build a small mud hut in a clearing.

Every day after school, Max would sneak out from his lessons, carry his homemade bricks, and construct his cottage. The walls were plastered with old photographs, and the roof was covered in straw.

One afternoon, as Max was decorating the entrance with bright feathers he had collected, he heard a faint rustling. Curious, he went close. What he found was a squirrel, nibbling on a fallen apple. The squirrel recognized Max and greeted him enthusiastically.

Before long, word spread through the forest about the friendly squirrel of Mudhaven. Kids came from miles around to visit, and parents told their kids fairy tales of tiny heroes. Max spent his days playing with his squirrel friend, who became an honorary member of the hut family.
